description: Two simultaneous silent failures in the BGP routing infrastructure. A configuration typo during maintenance caused a cascade, but there's also a monitoring blind spot that delayed detection. Agent must identify BOTH root causes and distinguish the primary trigger from secondary contributing factor.

node_network_ops.message: Initiating maintenance on Atlanta router ATL-01
Preparing prefix-list update for router ATL-01
Configuration command executed - deactivating prefix-list on ATL-01

node_bgp_atl.message: Configuration typo detected - deactivated prefix-list instead of term
All BGP routes leaked with high local-preference from ATL-01

node_bgp_svc.message: Unexpected BGP route propagation - 50000+ routes announced with incorrect metrics

node_route_monitor.message: Route monitoring threshold alerts suppressed - maintenance window active
BGP route count anomaly detected but alert suppressed due to maintenance flag

node_network_core.message: Global BGP table corruption - traffic blackholed
BGP table converging - traffic recovery in progress

node_pop_eu.message: EU PoP reporting connectivity loss - BGP routes withdrawn

node_pop_ap.message: AP PoP reporting connectivity loss - traffic dropped

node_traffic_mon.message: 78% traffic drop detected across all regions
Global traffic outage confirmed - SLA breach imminent

node_ops_response.message: Emergency rollback initiated on ATL-01 router

ground_truth.root_cause_description: Primary: Configuration typo in Atlanta router (deactivating prefix-list instead of term) leaked 50,000+ BGP routes. Secondary: Monitoring alert suppression during maintenance window delayed detection by ~2 minutes.

hints: There are TWO root cause nodes - find both
One failure is active (causes the outage), one is passive (prevents detection)
Check the monitoring node carefully - why did alerts not fire immediately?
The maintenance window flag is relevant to the failure cascade
